core_mechanism: maximum-entropy reconstruction of a positive spectral density from noisy integral-transform (correlator) data — i.e. regularized Bayesian inversion of an ill-posed inverse problem by selecting the least-biased distribution consistent with the measured constraints
brief_reason: The abstract states the spectral functions (peak positions, widths, and continua) are extracted from measured plane correlation functions using the maximum entropy method, which is exactly maximum-entropy inference used to invert an ill-posed spectral-reconstruction problem.
